topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sterile white of the testing room seemed to press in on Clara. She stared at the printout, the lines of genetic markers swimming before her eyes, each one a verdict. Her breath hitched, a shallow, ragged sound in the quiet office. Her hands, usually steady when she was coding, trembled slightly as she reread the confirmation. It wasn't a shock so much as a profound, dizzying realignment of her entire reality. She pressed the paper against her chest, feeling the frantic rhythm of her own heart hammering against her ribs. The air in the room suddenly felt too thin, too sharp. When the counselor finally spoke, her voice emerged as a reedy whisper, "I... I need a moment." She couldn't meet anyone's gaze; the world felt tilted on its axis.

[story 2]

Mark sat by the window, the afternoon sun doing little to warm the chill that had settled deep in his core. The results lay spread across his worn wooden desk, a collection of complex symbols that suddenly held the weight of an entire, unwritten history. He picked up a strand of hair from the sample kit, his fingers brushing against it with an almost fearful reverence. A strange pressure built behind his eyes, a hot, prickly sensation that made his vision swim. He pushed himself up from the chair, needing distance. He walked to the edge of the room, staring out at the familiar street, but everything looked alien now—the pavement, the passing cars, the very air. He leaned against the cool glass, taking slow, deliberate breaths, trying to anchor himself in a place that suddenly felt profoundly unstable.

[story 3]

Elara kept running the sequence over and over in her mind, a loop of impossible data that refused to resolve into anything sensible. When the final report appeared on the screen, the sound of the fluorescent lights above seemed to amplify into a deafening hum. Her fingers flew across the keyboard, not in productive motion, but in a desperate attempt to erase the information. A wave of nausea washed over her, making the room spin. She dropped her stylus; it clattered loudly on the desk, a jarring punctuation mark in the sudden silence. Elara stood up abruptly, needing to move, needing to physically escape the confines of the space where this truth had materialized. She walked toward the door, her movements jerky and uncontrolled, her entire body vibrating with an unfamiliar, intense energy.
